entryPoint.notes: Bound to <form onSubmit> at LoginForm.jsx:95. Calls e.preventDefault(), reads this.state.email and this.state.password, then delegates to AuthContext.login(email, password).

calls.notes: Returns { token, refreshToken } on success (HTTP 200). Returns HTTP 400 if email or password absent; HTTP 401 { error: 'Invalid credentials' } on wrong credentials.

serverHandler.notes: Validates against hardcoded ADMIN_EMAIL='admin@docvault.local' and ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH (bcrypt of 'docvault123'). No database access. Issues access token (1h) and refresh token (7d) via jwt.sign.

calls.notes: Payload: { userId: 'abc-123', email, role: 'admin' }. Signed with config.jwtSecret. Expiry: 1h.
Payload: { userId: 'abc-123', email, type: 'refresh' }. Signed with config.jwtSecret. Expiry: 7d.

BUG (FR-007): backend returns { session: { userId, email, createdAt } } instead of { token, refreshToken }. The following line (AuthContext.jsx:75) calls refreshResponse.data.token.split('.') — token is undefined — throwing 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading split)'. Execution falls to the catch block.

serverHandler.notes: BUG: verifies the refresh token with jwt.verify (correct) then returns { session: { userId, email, createdAt } } — a copy-paste from /session/login that was never corrected. Should return { token, refreshToken }.
